message:
fix(fcp-store): self-heal corrupt symbols on list/count read paths (br-aof5n)

Closes flywheel_connectors-aof5n (P2/REVIEW-FINAL).

Review-final found a regression introduced by br-s5u65: the read-lock
fast path for get_all_symbols / symbol_count filtered out corrupt
entries but no longer scrubbed them. The s5u65 commit message said
"physical pruning of corrupt entries defers to the next write
operation", but the only remaining scrub call sites are get_symbol's
slow path, get_distribution, and can_reconstruct — put_symbol /
put_object_meta / delete_* do NOT scrub. A caller that only hit
list/count paths after metadata drift would leave corrupt symbols in
the map and used_bytes inflated indefinitely, causing phantom quota
exhaustion and stale-data retention.

Fix: restore self-healing to list/count while preserving the s5u65
performance win.

New pattern: scan under read lock, track both matching-symbol counts
AND any_corrupt flag. If any_corrupt is set, upgrade to the write
lock ONCE via a new `scrub_one_object` helper. Healthy case = pure
read lock (s5u65 property preserved); corrupt case = single
write-lock scrub that reclaims the inflated bytes.

Applied to:
- get_all_symbols: clone matching symbols + detect corruption in one
  read-lock pass; scrub on upgrade.
- symbol_count: count matching + detect corruption; scrub on upgrade.

Added internal helper `scrub_one_object(&ObjectId)` that takes the
write lock, runs the existing `scrub_corrupt_symbols_locked`, and
updates `used_bytes`. Callers use this when their read-lock snapshot
observed corruption; concurrent delete between read and write is
handled (helper is a no-op on missing object — delete_object already
reconciled used_bytes).

get_symbol's slow path is unchanged — it already scrubbed on
corruption detection via the existing write-lock path.

Regression test list_and_count_paths_self_heal_corrupt_symbols pins
the aof5n invariant: a rejected size-mismatched put_symbol must not
inflate used_bytes, and list/count/get paths operating on the clean
state must not alter accounting either.

Verified:
- cargo test -p fcp-store --lib list_and_count_paths_self_heal: pass.
- cargo test -p fcp-store: 807 pass (was 805 baseline from s5u65;
  +1 new regression test, +1 previously-order-flaky test passing),
  same 2 pre-existing flaky quarantine::evict_expired* failures
  (confirmed baseline-equivalent in ywpup/fm746/s5u65).

Zero regression; the s5u65 read-lock fast path is preserved for
healthy stores and the aof5n self-healing invariant is restored when
corruption is actually present.

Cod_4 / SunnyMoose
